skill.name: snapdom
skill.description: Guides developers in using snapDOM to capture styled HTML elements and export them as SVG, PNG, JPG, WebP, Canvas, or Blob outputs.
skill.summary: Use snapDOM to turn styled browser DOM elements into image exports.
skill.author: 2025Emma

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution, weak cryptography, and screen capture upload are false positives caused by markdown code fences, inline option names, and example script names. The real risk is legitimate but sensitive DOM capture behavior and optional CORS proxy use, which can expose page content or resource URLs if misused.

medium_findings.title: DOM Capture Can Expose Sensitive Page Content
medium_findings.description: The skill teaches capturing HTML elements and downloading image outputs. This is legitimate functionality, but users could capture confidential page content, tokens shown in the DOM, or private customer data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The capture and download behavior is directly documented. It is not malicious, but it has clear privacy impact when used on sensitive DOM content.

medium_findings.title: Optional CORS Proxy Can Disclose Resource Requests
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends proxy fallback for CORS-blocked assets. A proxy can receive requested asset URLs and potentially asset contents, so users should only use trusted proxies.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The CORS proxy option is explicit and network-mediated. The example uses a placeholder URL, so the concern is misuse risk rather than confirmed exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Shell Execution Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The external command detections are markdown fences, inline option names, package installation commands, or development commands. No executable script file or user-input command construction was found in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are documentation examples and markdown formatting. I found no code path that executes shell input from the skill files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits do not show MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak algorithm usage. The flagged lines are descriptive text and a variable name inside examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines do not contain cryptographic operations. The scanner appears to have matched substrings in normal snapDOM documentation.

low_findings.title: Screen Capture Upload Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged line names a possible helper script for batch screenshots. It does not contain upload logic, an endpoint, or instructions to transmit captures externally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a documentation bullet, not implementation code. No evidence found for upload behavior in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and CDN URLs
low_findings.description: The skill includes official project, package, CDN, and placeholder proxy URLs. These are expected for documentation, but CDN loading and proxy examples should be reviewed by users before production use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs match documented installation and reference material. The residual risk is third-party dependency or proxy trust, not hidden network behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive DOM-to-Image Export Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Capturing and downloading DOM elements can preserve sensitive visible data. Users should avoid confidential pages and redact secrets before export.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented behavior is explicit and central to the skill. Risk depends on the user-selected target element.

dangerous_patterns.title: CORS Proxy Option
dangerous_patterns.description: Proxy fallback can route asset requests through a third party. Only trusted proxy endpoints should be used.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The proxy option is documented directly. The example does not prove malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Browser teams need reliable exports of styled HTML without rebuilding layouts by hand. This skill shows how to use snapDOM for accurate SVG and raster captures.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to install snapDOM with npm, yarn, or CDN imports.
Shows how to export elements as SVG, PNG, JPG, WebP, Canvas, or Blob.
Documents options for scale, width, height, fonts, shadows, transforms, and exclusions.
Covers CORS proxy fallback for blocked image and font resources.
Describes plugin hooks for custom export workflows and transformations.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for missing styles, fonts, CORS failures, and performance issues.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include runnable helper scripts.
It depends on browser APIs and is not a server-side renderer.
Cross-origin assets may require a trusted proxy or same-origin hosting.
Very large or complex elements may need lower scale values or smaller captures.

use_cases.title: Export UI States for Product Reviews
use_cases.description: Capture styled components, dashboards, or modals as images for design review and documentation.

use_cases.title: Generate Social Preview Images
use_cases.description: Render HTML templates at fixed dimensions and export them as shareable image assets.

use_cases.title: Compare Visual Outputs During Testing
use_cases.description: Create repeatable captures for checking layout changes across states, themes, or breakpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Capture One Element
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single screenshot export.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to use snapDOM to capture one element on my page as a PNG. Include the required import and basic call.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Export Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Adjust image size and quality settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure snapDOM for a high-resolution export with fixed dimensions, embedded fonts, and good PNG or JPG quality.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Missing Assets
prompt_templates.scenario: Debug CORS, fonts, and missing styles.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my snapDOM capture flow and suggest fixes for missing fonts, blocked images, and styles that do not appear in the output.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Export Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with exclusions and custom processing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a snapDOM-based export pipeline for multiple UI states. Include exclusions, scaling, proxy safety, and plugin extension points.

output_examples.input: A request to capture a dashboard widget for release notes.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan that selects PNG export, waits for fonts, excludes controls, and downloads the final asset.

output_examples.input: A report that captured images are missing external fonts.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering font loading, embedded fonts, same-origin assets, and browser console errors.

output_examples.input: A team wants social preview images from HTML templates.
output_examples.output: A workflow using fixed dimensions, controlled scale, SVG-first review, and final raster export for publishing.

best_practices: Capture only the smallest element that contains the needed visual content.
Wait for fonts, images, and layout changes to finish before exporting.
Use trusted same-origin assets or a vetted proxy for cross-origin resources.
anti_patterns: Do not capture pages that show secrets, tokens, private records, or customer data.
Do not rely on public CORS proxies for production exports.
Do not use very high scale values on large pages without testing memory and performance.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you use snapDOM to export styled browser elements as image files or canvas outputs.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The scanned files are documentation and examples, not runnable automation scripts.

faq.question: Which output formats are covered?
faq.answer: The skill covers SVG, PNG, JPG, WebP, Canvas, and Blob outputs.

faq.question: Can it capture external fonts and images?
faq.answer: It can help, but cross-origin resources may need same-origin hosting or a trusted proxy.

faq.question: Is it safe to use on private dashboards?
faq.answer: Use care. Captures can include visible private data, so redact sensitive content before exporting.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The static high-risk hits were dismissed, but DOM capture and proxy use require privacy warnings.
